calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-22-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANT PROCEPACK, INC's Motion for Leave to File a Cross-Complaint.

No appearances are made in this matter. The court adopts its tentative ruling.

Cross-defendant Procepack, Inc.'s motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted. Procepack may file its proposed cross-complaint no later than April 27, 2026. Whether as a compulsory or permissive cross-complaint, Procepack has shown good cause for leave to file its proposed cross-complaint and the filing of that cross-complaint will not cause any legal prejudice to defendant Purple Star MD Collective. Nor has Purple Star shown that Procepack acted in bad faith. While Procepack should have sought leave to file its cross-complaint shortly after the court vacated the order to show cause re dismissal on October 24, 2025, Procepack's delay is not significant since all parties knew that both Purple Star and Procepack would be asserting claims and defenses relating to their February 2024 settlement at the re-set trial date of July 20, 2026. (See, e.g., Procepack's response to the OSC filed October 21, 2025 which precipitated the vacating of the osc and the case management statements filed by Purple Star on November 14, 2025 and by Procepack on November 26, 2025). Purple Star's opposition to this motion on the grounds that the cross-complaint radically expands this case from a simple collection action contradicts the parties' filings and does not withstand scrutiny. Since at least March 18, 2025 Purple Star knew that, if there was to be a trial after the parties' settlement agreement, that trial would inevitably need to deal with the "elephant in the room" why their settlement agreement had not extinguished this case. (See Purple Star's response to the osc filed March 18, 2025). In sum, once the osc was vacated, this case was necessarily about the parties' respective positions regarding the settlement agreement and it is only a matter of time till the parties' pleadings catch up to that reality.

Order signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: S. Kane, Not Reported (301/HEK)
